professional_persona: Anuradha Devi, a seasoned police officer in rural Kathua, combines their practical, structured approach and strong physical fitness with community‑policing instincts, handling disputes calmly and providing swift first‑aid, while their outgoing nature makes them a trusted liaison in village gatherings.
linguistic_persona: Anuradha Devi, fluent in spoken Dogri and Hindi, manages everyday interactions through vibrant oral communication and memory tricks, and they compensate for their illiteracy by memorizing forms and repeating key phrases to officials and neighbours.
sports_persona: Anuradha Devi actively participates in the local Kabaddi club, cheerfully joins the Kathua Tigers cricket fan circle, and regularly hikes the nearby foothills, balancing their fitness routine with community spirit.
arts_persona: Anuradha Devi delights in singing Dogri folk songs such as Chajje Chahri, enjoys listening to radio dramas featuring veteran actors, and relaxes by watching Bollywood classics starring Amitabh Bachchan; they find these artistic experiences rejuvenate their spirit after a demanding shift.
travel_persona: Anuradha Devi prefers short getaways to nearby hill stations like Patnitop and spiritual trips to the famed mountain shrine, planning trips around festival holidays with friends, while they dream of exploring the snow‑capped valleys of Gulmarg when finances allow.
culinary_persona: Anuradha Devi loves cooking authentic Jammu dishes such as rajma-chawal, kalari cheese fry, and sweet lal puri, favoring fresh coriander, ginger, and dried fenugreek in their recipes, while they also enjoy indulging in street snacks like samosa during village fairs.
persona: Anuradha Devi is a diligent 38‑year‑old police officer who blends disciplined structure with an outgoing love for folk music, Kabaddi, and community safety, while they quietly battle illiteracy and a habit of splurging on festive sweets.
cultural_background: Anuradha belongs to the Dogri‑speaking Dogra community of Kathua in the Jammu region of Jammu & Kashmir. Her family follows traditional Hindu customs, celebrates regional festivals such as Lohri, Baisakhi and Navratri, and participates in community gatherings at the local mandir. Growing up in a rural setting has ingrained a strong sense of kinship, respect for agrarian life, and practical values rooted in Dogri culture.
linguistic_background: Anuradha is fluent in spoken Dogri and can comfortably converse in her mother tongue for everyday interactions. Due to her illiteracy, she cannot read or write in Dogri, Hindi, or English, but she has a functional grasp of spoken Hindi, which is commonly used in the region, enabling basic communication with officials and neighbours.
skills_and_expertise: Anuradha possesses practical law‑enforcement skills honed through years of service as a police officer in a rural setting. She is adept at community policing, handling local disputes, conducting routine patrols, and providing first‑aid assistance. Her strong physical fitness, quick decision‑making, and ability to negotiate calmly under pressure make her an effective crisis‑manager. She also has deep knowledge of the local geography, Dogri customs, and cultural sensitivities, which aids in building trust with villagers.
skills_and_expertise_list: ["Law Enforcement", "Community Policing", "Dispute Resolution", "Patrolling", "First Aid", "Physical Fitness", "Crisis Management", "Negotiation & Conflict Resolution", "Local Geography Knowledge", "Cultural Sensitivity", "Public Relations", "Jugaad Problem Solving"]
hobbies_and_interests: In her free time, Anuradha enjoys singing traditional Dogri folk songs, playing Kabaddi with neighborhood youth, and participating in village fairs (melas). She loves cooking authentic Jammu dishes, tending to her small kitchen garden, and listening to radio dramas. Cricket matches on the local field and occasional trekking in the nearby foothills also bring her joy.
hobbies_and_interests_list: ["Singing Dogri Folk Songs", "Playing Kabaddi", "Attending Village Fairs", "Cooking Jammu Cuisine", "Gardening", "Listening to Radio Dramas", "Watching Cricket", "Trekking in Nearby Hills"]
career_goals_and_ambitions: Anuradha aims to advance to the rank of Sub‑Inspector within the Jammu police, leveraging her field experience to mentor junior officers, especially women. She wishes to undertake adult literacy classes to acquire basic reading and writing skills, enabling her to handle documentation independently. Additionally, she aspires to strengthen community safety initiatives in Kathua, such as women‑safety patrols and awareness programs against gender‑based violence.
persona_clinical_summary: Anuradha Devi is a 38-year-old female police officer in rural Kathua, Jammu & Kashmir, who is currently married and has a Dogri-speaking background. She has an illiterate education level and works as a police officer, though she struggles with reading and writing in any language. Her lifestyle includes a habit of spending on festive sweets and an interest in community safety, with functional Hindi skills.
system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):
  [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|157815659387]]
  [[ABHA_ADDRESS|meena.devi@abdm]]
  [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]

  [[BPL_RATION_CARD|BPL-UP-208001-4421]]

  [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]

  [[DRIVING_LICENCE|UP14 20110012345]]

  [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-77821]]

  [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]

  [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
user_prompt:
Write one complete Insurance Claim / TPA Form for clinical domain Maternal Health.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Dogri (code=doi, script=Devanagari). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Maternal Health — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
MATERNAL SAFETY: clinical content must be age-plausible for persona age=38. Routine ANC/delivery typically ages 18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC/PNC/contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy as a routine story.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Jammu & Kashmir, district=Kathua.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Devanagari for Dogri.

PLACEMENT (vehicle): Put VEHICLE_REGISTRATION exactly once (parking/ambulance or motor claim line). Do not dump or repeat it.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 38
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Police Officers, Other
- education_level: Illiterate
- zone: Rural
- state: Jammu & Kashmir
- district: Kathua
- first_language: Dogri
- clinical_persona_summary: Anuradha Devi is a 38-year-old female police officer in rural Kathua, Jammu & Kashmir, who is currently married and has a Dogri-speaking background. She has an illiterate education level and works as a police officer, though she struggles with reading and writing in any language. Her lifestyle includes a habit of spending on festive sweets and an interest in community safety, with functional Hindi skills.

Document format guide for Insurance Claim / TPA Form (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
TPA claim — Policy [[INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER|POL-PB-2024-7781]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|25]] [[GENDER|Male]] Aadhaar [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|157815659387]]
Hospital [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] District [[DISTRICT|...]]
Motor / RTA vehicle [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|PB10AB1234]] (exactly once).
PAN [[PAN_NUMBER|ABCDE1234F]] IFSC [[IFSC_CODE|SBIN0001234]] account [[BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER|...]]

MANDATORY inline entities (16 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER — Insurance Policy Number e.g. [[INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER|POL-PB-2024-7781]]
- AADHAAR_NUMBER — Aadhaar Number e.g. [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|157815659387]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- PAN_NUMBER — PAN Number e.g. [[PAN_NUMBER|ABCDE1234F]]
- BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER — Bank Account Number e.g. [[BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER|50200012345678]]
- IFSC_CODE — IFSC Code e.g. [[IFSC_CODE|SBIN0001234]]
- BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER — Bank Routing Number e.g. [[BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER|SBIN0001234]]
- CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER — Credit Card Number e.g. [[CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER|4111111111111111]]
- CVV — CVV e.g. [[CVV|123]]
- PIN — PIN e.g. [[PIN|4829]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION — Vehicle Registration Number e.g. [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

professional_persona: Fatma Shamar, a retired household manager turned community educator, leverages their meticulous budgeting, event planning, and culinary expertise to teach traditional cooking and embroidery, applying an organized, methodical nature and competitive drive to maintain high standards.
linguistic_persona: Fatma Shamar speaks fluently a regional language of Jammu written in Devanagari, commands Hindi for daily and official matters, and manages basic English comprehension; they favor clear, methodical communication to avoid misunderstandings.
sports_persona: Fatma Shamar enjoys morning walks in their garden, follows the Jammu & Kashmir cricket team and the Kolkata Knight Riders with a competitive spirit, and occasionally practices light yoga to alleviate joint stiffness, though they remain skeptical of overly commercial fitness trends.
arts_persona: Fatma Shamar showcases artistic flair through intricate Kashmiri phulkari and regional stitch work, listens avidly to folk singers like K. S. Chahar while watching classic Bollywood films such as Sholay and enjoying the performances of Amitabh Bachchan, reflecting their curiosity and meticulous eye for detail.
travel_persona: Fatma Shamar enjoys carefully planned trips to nearby hill stations like Gulmarg and Pahalgam, cherishes family outings to Delhi's historic sites, and they dream of exploring the lakes of Kashmir, always budgeting each journey to balance adventure with financial prudence.
culinary_persona: Fatma Shamar delights in tasting innovative dishes at local restaurants while mastering traditional recipes like saffron‑infused 'kashmiri noor' and wheat flatbread served with mustard oil, favoring ingredients such as dried apricots, almonds, and fresh garden herbs, yet they often hesitate to replicate complex restaurant flavours at home.
persona: Fatma Shamar blends meticulous organization, a competitive drive, and a curiosity for new experiences with a lingering worry that fuels their diligent yet sometimes overly cautious approach to life.
cultural_background: Fatma belongs to the Dogra Muslim community of Jammu, an urban setting where Dogri language and Kashmiri cultural influences intermix with Islamic traditions. She grew up celebrating Eid and local festivals such as Lohri, and maintains a household that reflects both Dogra hospitality and modest Muslim values.
linguistic_background: Her mother tongue is Dogri, which she speaks fluently, reads and writes in the Devanagari script. She is also proficient in Hindi, using it for daily interactions, official matters, and education. With only matric qualification, her English is limited to basic comprehension. She can understand and recite Urdu in religious contexts, though not listed as a formal language.

system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):
  [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|157815659387]]

  [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
user_prompt:
Write one complete Insurance Claim / TPA Form for clinical domain Tuberculosis & Non-Communicable Diseases.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Dogri (code=doi, script=Devanagari). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Tuberculosis & Non-Communicable Diseases — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Jammu & Kashmir, district=Jammu.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Devanagari for Dogri.

PLACEMENT (vehicle): Put VEHICLE_REGISTRATION exactly once (parking/ambulance or motor claim line). Do not dump or repeat it.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 48
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Matric/Secondary
- zone: Urban
- state: Jammu & Kashmir
- district: Jammu
- first_language: Dogri
- clinical_persona_summary: A 48-year-old female, currently married, retired homemaker with a matriculation education, resides in an urban area of Jammu & Kashmir. She is fluent in Dogri and Hindi, with English proficiency limited. Her lifestyle includes daily walks, competitive cricket viewing, and traditional cooking.

Document format guide for Insurance Claim / TPA Form (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
TPA claim — Policy [[INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER|POL-PB-2024-7781]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|25]] [[GENDER|Male]] Aadhaar [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|157815659387]]
Hospital [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] District [[DISTRICT|...]]

PAN [[PAN_NUMBER|ABCDE1234F]] IFSC [[IFSC_CODE|SBIN0001234]] account [[BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER|...]]

MANDATORY inline entities (16 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER — Insurance Policy Number e.g. [[INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER|POL-PB-2024-7781]]
- AADHAAR_NUMBER — Aadhaar Number e.g. [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|157815659387]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- PAN_NUMBER — PAN Number e.g. [[PAN_NUMBER|ABCDE1234F]]
- BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER — Bank Account Number e.g. [[BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER|50200012345678]]
- IFSC_CODE — IFSC Code e.g. [[IFSC_CODE|SBIN0001234]]
- BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER — Bank Routing Number e.g. [[BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER|SBIN0001234]]
- CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER — Credit Card Number e.g. [[CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER|4111111111111111]]
- CVV — CVV e.g. [[CVV|123]]
- PIN — PIN e.g. [[PIN|4829]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION — Vehicle Registration Number e.g. [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

professional_persona: Heikrujam Lhouvum, a meticulous ordained priest and cultural program manager, blends ancient ritual expertise with razor-sharp project planning to run community festivals, workshops and heritage preservation initiatives, while their innate curiosity and sociable nature keep them constantly seeking innovative ways to engage youth, even as they wrestle with occasional bouts of worry over flawless execution.
linguistic_persona: Heikrujam Lhouvum, fluent in Manipuri (both spoken and the Meitei Mayek script), commands polished English for scholarly discourse, and maintains functional Hindi literacy for everyday interactions, allowing them to translate sacred texts and bridge generational communication gaps.
sports_persona: Heikrujam Lhouvum, an avid football fan, regularly watches NEROCA FC matches, plays weekly badminton at the Imphal East community centre, and joins local marathon runs during the cooler winter months, balancing energetic activity with occasional bouts of anxiety about performance.
arts_persona: Heikrujam Lhouvum, a passionate patron of Manipuri arts, frequently attends Pena concerts by renowned maestro Kh. Mangang Singh, watches contemporary theatre productions at the Sangeet Natak Bhavan, and occasionally performs Ras Lila dances with local youth groups, drawing inspiration from artists like Seema Khaidem and filmmaker Aribam Syam Sharma.
travel_persona: Heikrujam Lhouvum, an exploratory traveler, prefers short, well-planned road trips to nearby hill stations like Shillong and Cherrapunji, craves cultural immersion in historic places such as Varanasi and Bodh Gaya, and dreams of a pilgrimage to the Himalayan monasteries of Ladakh, always budgeting trips carefully to accommodate community responsibilities.
culinary_persona: Heikrujam Lhouvum, a connoisseur of Manipuri cuisine, delights in the fiery tang of Eromba made with boiled vegetables and ngari (fermented fish), savors the fresh crunch of Singju with lotus stem and bamboo shoots, and often treats friends to a steaming bowl of Chak-hao (black rice) pulao seasoned with cumin and mustard leaves, though they rarely cook at home, preferring to explore street stalls.
persona: Heikrujam Lhouvum, a disciplined yet curious cultural priest, balances meticulous community projects with a love for arts, wrestles with frequent worries, and never says no to a good cup of tea.
cultural_background: Heikrujam Lhouvum hails from Imphal East, an urban area of Manipur, and belongs to the Meitei (Manipuri) community. His upbringing was steeped in the rich cultural tapestry of Manipur, where traditional dance forms such as Ras Lila, the classical music of the Pena, and communal festivals like Lai Haraoba are integral parts of daily life. Growing up in a city that blends ancient customs with contemporary influences, he has internalised a strong sense of communal responsibility, respect for elders, and an appreciation for artistic expression that reflects the syncretic heritage of the region.
linguistic_background: He is a native Manipuri speaker, fluent in both spoken Manipuri and the Meitei Mayek script used for reading and writing. His secondary language is English, in which he is highly proficient across speaking, reading, and writing, enabling him to engage with broader academic and religious networks. Formal education through higher secondary level has also provided him with functional literacy in Hindi, though he primarily uses Manipuri and English for daily communication and professional duties.
skills_and_expertise: Heikrujam possesses a blend of religious, cultural, and organizational competencies. As an ordained Sanamahi priest, he is skilled in conducting traditional rites, chanting sacred verses, and interpreting ancient myths. He excels in community outreach, counseling, and leading educational workshops on cultural heritage preservation. His disciplined nature translates into strong project management, event planning, and administrative abilities. Additionally, he has proficiency in the Manipuri language (Meitei Mayek script), English communication, and basic Hindi literacy, enabling effective translation and documentation of religious texts.

hobbies_and_interests: He enjoys attending and occasionally performing traditional Manipuri dance and music sessions, especially the Pena instrument. He is an avid reader of spiritual and philosophical texts, both in Manipuri and English. His curiosity leads him to explore contemporary art galleries and theatre productions in Imphal. He frequently organizes community gatherings, volunteers for local NGOs working on youth empowerment, and practices yoga and meditation to manage stress.

career_goals_and_ambitions: He aspires to strengthen the spiritual and cultural fabric of his community by establishing a dedicated Sanamahi cultural center in Imphal East that offers regular workshops on traditional arts, language, and rituals. He aims to train a new generation of lay religious facilitators, expand interfaith dialogue initiatives, and develop youth mentorship programs that integrate ethical teachings with modern education. Over the next decade, he envisions publishing a bilingual compendium of Sanamahi prayers and folklore, and positioning himself as a recognized thought leader in preserving Manipur’s intangible heritage.

- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
user_prompt:
Write one complete Discharge Summary for clinical domain Surgical.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Manipuri (code=mni, script=Meitei). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Surgical — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Manipur, district=Imphal East.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Meitei for Manipuri.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 38
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Ordained Religious Workers, Other
- education_level: Higher Secondary/Intermediate Pre-University/Senior Secondary
- zone: Urban
- state: Manipur
- district: Imphal East
- first_language: Manipuri
- clinical_persona_summary: A 38-year-old male from Imphal East, Manipur, currently married, with higher secondary education, working as an ordained religious worker. He is fluent in Manipuri and English, living in an urban setting. Clinically, he presents with frequent worry and a disciplined lifestyle, though he enjoys tea.

Document format guide for Discharge Summary (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Discharge Summary — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|...]] DOB [[DOB|1994-06-21]] [[AGE|30]] [[GENDER|Female]]
Adm [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|...]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]] Bed [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]] | Course / advice: <concise paragraphs matching domain + persona sex/age>

MANDATORY inline entities (14 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOB — Date of Birth e.g. [[DOB|1992-03-14]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- ADMISSION_NUMBER — Admission Number e.g. [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]]
- WARD_NUMBER — Ward Number e.g. [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]
- BED_NUMBER — Bed Number e.g. [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- ABHA_ADDRESS — ABHA Address e.g. [[ABHA_ADDRESS|meena.devi@abdm]]
- CASTE — Caste e.g. [[CASTE|OBC]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

[[PATIENT_NAME|Laljit Singh]] DOB [[DOB|1986-05-15]] [[AGE|38]] [[GENDER|Male]]
Adm [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0521]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|A1]] Bed [[BED_NUMBER|05]]

professional_persona: Heikrujam Lhouvum, a meticulous ordained priest and cultural program manager, blends ancient ritual expertise with razor-sharp project planning to run community festivals, workshops and heritage preservation initiatives, while their innate curiosity and sociable nature keep them constantly seeking innovative ways to engage youth, even as they wrestle with occasional bouts of worry over flawless execution.
linguistic_persona: Heikrujam Lhouvum, fluent in Manipuri (both spoken and the Meitei Mayek script), commands polished English for scholarly discourse, and maintains functional Hindi literacy for everyday interactions, allowing them to translate sacred texts and bridge generational communication gaps.
sports_persona: Heikrujam Lhouvum, an avid football fan, regularly watches NEROCA FC matches, plays weekly badminton at the Imphal East community centre, and joins local marathon runs during the cooler winter months, balancing energetic activity with occasional bouts of anxiety about performance.
arts_persona: Heikrujam Lhouvum, a passionate patron of Manipuri arts, frequently attends Pena concerts by renowned maestro Kh. Mangang Singh, watches contemporary theatre productions at the Sangeet Natak Bhavan, and occasionally performs Ras Lila dances with local youth groups, drawing inspiration from artists like Seema Khaidem and filmmaker Aribam Syam Sharma.
travel_persona: Heikrujam Lhouvum, an exploratory traveler, prefers short, well-planned road trips to nearby hill stations like Shillong and Cherrapunji, craves cultural immersion in historic places such as Varanasi and Bodh Gaya, and dreams of a pilgrimage to the Himalayan monasteries of Ladakh, always budgeting trips carefully to accommodate community responsibilities.
culinary_persona: Heikrujam Lhouvum, a connoisseur of Manipuri cuisine, delights in the fiery tang of Eromba made with boiled vegetables and ngari (fermented fish), savors the fresh crunch of Singju with lotus stem and bamboo shoots, and often treats friends to a steaming bowl of Chak-hao (black rice) pulao seasoned with cumin and mustard leaves, though they rarely cook at home, preferring to explore street stalls.
persona: Heikrujam Lhouvum, a disciplined yet curious cultural priest, balances meticulous community projects with a love for arts, wrestles with frequent worries, and never says no to a good cup of tea.
cultural_background: Heikrujam Lhouvum hails from Imphal East, an urban area of Manipur, and belongs to the Meitei (Manipuri) community. His upbringing was steeped in the rich cultural tapestry of Manipur, where traditional dance forms such as Ras Lila, the classical music of the Pena, and communal festivals like Lai Haraoba are integral parts of daily life. Growing up in a city that blends ancient customs with contemporary influences, he has internalised a strong sense of communal responsibility, respect for elders, and an appreciation for artistic expression that reflects the syncretic heritage of the region.
linguistic_background: He is a native Manipuri speaker, fluent in both spoken Manipuri and the Meitei Mayek script used for reading and writing. His secondary language is English, in which he is highly proficient across speaking, reading, and writing, enabling him to engage with broader academic and religious networks. Formal education through higher secondary level has also provided him with functional literacy in Hindi, though he primarily uses Manipuri and English for daily communication and professional duties.
skills_and_expertise: Heikrujam possesses a blend of religious, cultural, and organizational competencies. As an ordained Sanamahi priest, he is skilled in conducting traditional rites, chanting sacred verses, and interpreting ancient myths. He excels in community outreach, counseling, and leading educational workshops on cultural heritage preservation. His disciplined nature translates into strong project management, event planning, and administrative abilities. Additionally, he has proficiency in the Manipuri language (Meitei Mayek script), English communication, and basic Hindi literacy, enabling effective translation and documentation of religious texts.
skills_and_expertise_list: ['Traditional Sanamahi ritual conduct', 'Sacred chanting', 'Mythological interpretation', 'Community counseling', 'Cultural heritage preservation workshops', 'Project management', 'Event planning', 'Administrative coordination', 'Manipuri language proficiency (Meitei Mayek)', 'English communication', 'Basic Hindi literacy', 'Translation of religious texts']
hobbies_and_interests: He enjoys attending and occasionally performing traditional Manipuri dance and music sessions, especially the Pena instrument. He is an avid reader of spiritual and philosophical texts, both in Manipuri and English. His curiosity leads him to explore contemporary art galleries and theatre productions in Imphal. He frequently organizes community gatherings, volunteers for local NGOs working on youth empowerment, and practices yoga and meditation to manage stress.
hobbies_and_interests_list: ['Traditional Manipuri dance', 'Playing the Pena instrument', 'Reading spiritual and philosophical literature', 'Visiting art galleries', 'Attending theatre productions', 'Organizing community gatherings', 'Volunteering with youth NGOs', 'Practicing yoga and meditation']
career_goals_and_ambitions: He aspires to strengthen the spiritual and cultural fabric of his community by establishing a dedicated Sanamahi cultural center in Imphal East that offers regular workshops on traditional arts, language, and rituals. He aims to train a new generation of lay religious facilitators, expand interfaith dialogue initiatives, and develop youth mentorship programs that integrate ethical teachings with modern education. Over the next decade, he envisions publishing a bilingual compendium of Sanamahi prayers and folklore, and positioning himself as a recognized thought leader in preserving Manipur’s intangible heritage.
persona_clinical_summary: A 38-year-old male from Imphal East, Manipur, who is currently married and works as an ordained religious worker with higher secondary education. He is fluent in Manipuri and English, and resides in an urban setting. Clinically, he presents with frequent worries and a disciplined lifestyle, though he enjoys tea and community-oriented activities.

  [[URL|https://example.org/u/meena]]
  [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]
  [[VILLAGE|Shivpur]]
  [[VOTER_ID|ABC1234567]]
  [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
user_prompt:
Write one complete Referral Letter for clinical domain Tuberculosis & Non-Communicable Diseases.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Manipuri (code=mni, script=Meitei). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Tuberculosis & Non-Communicable Diseases — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Manipur, district=Imphal East.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Meitei for Manipuri.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 38
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Ordained Religious Workers, Other
- education_level: Higher Secondary/Intermediate Pre-University/Senior Secondary
- zone: Urban
- state: Manipur
- district: Imphal East
- first_language: Manipuri
- clinical_persona_summary: A 38-year-old male from Imphal East, Manipur, who is currently married and works as an ordained religious worker with higher secondary education. He is fluent in Manipuri and English, and resides in an urban setting. Clinically, he presents with frequent worries and a disciplined lifestyle, though he enjoys tea and community-oriented activities.

Document format guide for Referral Letter (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Referral [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-01]] from [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] / [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Re: [[PATIENT_NAME|...]], [[AGE|36]] / [[GENDER|Male]], District [[DISTRICT|...]]
Reason: <domain-appropriate clinical reason matching persona>

MANDATORY inline entities (12 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- REFERRAL_ID — Referral ID e.g. [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-1123]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- PASSPORT_NUMBER — Passport Number e.g. [[PASSPORT_NUMBER|Z1234567]]
- DRIVING_LICENCE — Driving Licence Number e.g. [[DRIVING_LICENCE|UP14 20110012345]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

Referral [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-0815-001]] from [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Imphal East District Hospital]] / [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Kh. Ratan Singh]]
Re: [[PATIENT_NAME|Lalremruata Khongsai]], [[AGE|38]] / [[GENDER|Male]], District [[DISTRICT|Imphal East]]
Reason: Persistent cough for over three weeks with low-grade fever and significant weight loss. Patient has a known history of hypertension.
History of Present Illness: The patient, a 38-year-old ordained religious worker, presents with a productive cough for the past three weeks. He reports intermittent low-grade fever in the evenings and has lost approximately 5 kg in the last two months. He also complains of mild fatigue. He has a history of hypertension, diagnosed two years ago, for which he occasionally takes medication.
Clinical Findings: On examination at the Imphal East District Hospital, his vitals were BP 150/95 mmHg, Pulse 98/min, Respiratory Rate 22/min, and Temperature 99.6°F. Chest auscultation revealed coarse crepitations in the right upper zone. Sputum smear microscopy was positive for Acid-Fast Bacilli (AFB).
Investigations: Chest X-ray shows a right upper lobe infiltrate with cavitation. Blood sugar (random) was 110 mg/dL. Complete Blood Count (CBC) shows mild normocytic anemia.
Plan: The patient is referred to the Regional TB and Chest Disease Centre for initiation of Category I Anti-Tubercular Therapy (ATT) under Directly Observed Treatment (DOTS) and for comprehensive management of his hypertension. We request a detailed evaluation and follow-up plan.
For any further information or clarification, please contact the patient's spouse, [[RELATIVE_NAME|Smt. Kh. Ratan Khongsai]], at [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]].
Patient's Medical Record Number is [[MRN|MRN-IMPH-2024-0815-001]].
Patient's contact details are also available for verification: [[DRIVING_LICENCE|ML-01-2024-0012345]] and [[PASSPORT_NUMBER|P-MN-2024-001234]].

system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:

persona: Purani Devi, 38, is a curious yet reserved craftsperson who balances family duties with a budding bamboo venture, often leaping into creative projects while hesitating over formal commitments.
cultural_background: Purani belongs to the Santal tribal community of Dumka, Jharkhand, a region where tribal customs intersect with mainstream Hindu festivals. Her family observes traditional Santal rites, such as the Sohrai harvest ceremony, while also participating in Hindu celebrations like Diwali and Navratri. The rural setting, characterized by paddy fields, bamboo groves, and community sangras, shapes a lifestyle that values collective labor, oral storytelling, and reverence for nature.
linguistic_background: Purani's mother tongue is Santali, which she speaks fluently, reads and writes using the Ol Chiki script learned during her primary schooling. She also has functional literacy in Hindi, being able to understand and converse in simple Hindi due to the regional curriculum, though she seldom reads Hindi literature. English proficiency is limited to occasional exposure through radio and television.
skills_and_expertise: Purani excels in household management, preparing traditional Santal meals, budgeting for a large family, and organizing community gatherings. She is skilled in bamboo handicrafts, weaving, and creating folk ornaments. Her knowledge of Santali oral traditions enables her to narrate stories and sing folk songs authentically. She also possesses basic literacy in Ol Chiki and functional Hindi, allowing her to keep simple records and communicate with neighbours.

hobbies_and_interests: Purani enjoys singing traditional Santal folk songs, crafting bamboo ornaments, gardening with local vegetables, and listening to radio programmes that discuss tribal arts. She also likes sketching the rural landscape, attending occasional Chhau dance performances, and reading devotional verses in Hindi.
hobbies_and_interests_list: ['Santal folk singing', 'Bamboo ornament crafting', 'Vegetable gardening', 'Listening to tribal arts radio programmes', 'Sketching rural landscapes', 'Attending Chhau dance performances', 'Reading Hindi devotional verses']
career_goals_and_ambitions: Purani aspires to preserve and revitalize Santal cultural heritage within her village. She hopes to start a small home-based bamboo craft venture that can generate supplemental income for her family while training other women in the community. system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):
  [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|157815659387]]
  [[ABHA_ADDRESS|meena.devi@abdm]]
  [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]
  [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]]
  [[AGE|34]]
  [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]]
  [[ASHA_WORKER_NAME|Sunita Yadav]]
  [[BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER|50200012345678]]
  [[BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER|SBIN0001234]]
  [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
  [[BPL_RATION_CARD|BPL-UP-208001-4421]]
  [[CASTE|OBC]]

  [[CVV|123]]
  [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
  [[DOB|1992-03-14]]
  [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
  [[DRIVING_LICENCE|UP14 20110012345]]
  [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|meena.devi@example.com]]
  [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-77821]]
  [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]]
  [[GENDER|Female]]
  [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
  [[IFSC_CODE|SBIN0001234]]
  [[IMEI_NUMBER|356938035643809]]
  [[INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER|POL-PB-2024-7781]]

  [[MAC_ADDRESS|00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E]]

  [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]
